articles.title: Brockton woman identified as Lakeville drowning victim
articles.description:
The woman died after falling off a jet ski on Long Pond in Lakeville. Authorities said she was not wearing a life jacket at the time.

The post Brockton woman identified as Lakeville drowning victim appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Brockton woman drowned after falling off a jet ski in Long Pond, Lakeville.
Authorities confirmed she was not wearing a life jacket during the incident.
This tragedy highlights the importance of safety measures on local waterways.

articles.title: As teen sports betting rises, Mass. rolls out a new classroom lesson
articles.description:
A pilot program is teaching Massachusetts high school students about the risks of online gambling and how to recognize problem betting.

summary: Massachusetts launches a pilot program teaching high school students about online gambling risks.
The program aims to educate students on recognizing problem betting behaviors.
Local schools are addressing rising teen sports betting concerns through this initiative.

articles.title: Dating back to 1776, the Potter Mansion survived 250 years. Its clock is still keeping time.
articles.description:
Revolutionary War captain John Potter started building the Potter Mansion starting in 1776 in North Brookfield. Today, it’s open as a museum in West Springfield.

The post Dating back to 1776, the Potter Mansion survived 250 years. Its clock is still keeping time. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Potter Mansion, built in 1776, is now a museum in West Springfield.
The mansion was constructed by Revolutionary War captain John Potter.
Boston residents can explore 250 years of history at this local landmark.
